name: Chipotle-Ancho Chili Pesto
recipeIngredient: Canned chipotle chilies, 2 each (in adobo sauce)
recipeInstructions:
1. If you prefer a milder sauce, use only one chipotle chili in this recipe; if you like your food super spicy, use two.
2. Remove stems and seeds from ancho chilies. Place the chilies in a large bowl. Cover with boiling water. Let stand until soft, about 30 minutes. Drain, reserving 1 tablespoon of the soaking liquid.
3. Roast red bell pepper over gas flame or in broiler until blackened on all sides. Seal in paper bag and let stand 15 minutes. Peel, stem, and seed the pepper.
4. In a food processor, blend ancho chilies, 1 tablespoon of the soaking liquid, roasted bell pepper, chipotle chilies, pine nuts, fresh lemon juice, and garlic until almost smooth.
5. With the machine running, gradually add olive oil in 1/4 cup increments; process until smooth. If the pesto is dry, mix in more oil by spoonfuls. Season with salt.
6. The recipe makes about 1-1/3 cups. It can be made ahead: Press plastic wrap onto the surface of the pesto, cover, and refrigerate up to 2 days or freeze up to 1 week. Bring to room temperature before using.
